description: Conservative generic absorber family starter entries for Extension 2 exploratory mode. All entries are generic. Values are conservative estimates for trade space exploration only. Do not treat as measured material performance or flight qualification.

entries.label: Broadband Mid-IR Absorber (Generic)
notes: Represents a generic high-emissivity mid-IR coating class. Absorptivity and emissivity are similar for broadband non-selective surfaces (Kirchhoff balance near unity). Conservative estimate for trade use.
Actual coating performance depends on specific material, substrate preparation, and operating temperature. Do not use for design commitment without bench characterization.

entries.label: Narrowband Mid-IR Absorber (Generic)
notes: Represents the concept of a narrowband selective absorber tuned to the 8–10 µm range. Selectivity claims (high absorptivity, low emissivity in out-of-band region) are concept-level only for generic entry.
Real narrowband selective absorbers require detailed spectral characterization. Values are placeholder-adjacent; treat as directional only.

entries.label: Ceramic Selective Absorber (Generic)
notes: Ceramic emitter/absorber materials (e.g. SiC, BN composites, rare-earth oxides) have been demonstrated to exhibit selective emissivity behavior in the mid-IR. Values are representative of a generic class, not a specific commercial coating.
High-temperature operating range is relevant for hot-island and regen-store applications. Radiation stability noted as low sensitivity based on heritage refractory ceramic analogs.

entries.label: Refractory Receiver Surface (Generic)
notes: Refractory ceramics and carbon-based receiver surfaces are used in concentrating solar and high-temperature process systems. Values represent generic refractory absorber behavior.
Applicable to hot-island and solar-polish scenarios only. At these temperatures, radiative emission becomes significant; net thermal gain requires careful geometry accounting.
